x-enumeration-literals.name: Fixed Conversion Redemption Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A predefined, constant rate is used to convert rewards into value.

Comment:
Example: 100 points = €1

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tiered Conversion Redemption Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description: The conversion rate varies based on thresholds or tiers.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Dynamic Conversion Redemption Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The value of rewards fluctuates based on external or internal factors.

Influenced by demand, time, partner pricing, or promotions.

Comment:
Example: Travel points worth more during off-peak periods.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Market-Linked Conversion Redemption Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption value is linked to real-time market prices or partner valuations.

Comment:
Example: Points converted to airline tickets based on current ticket price.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Fixed Value Redemption Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rewards correspond to specific predefined items or services, not a formula.

Comment:
Example: 10,000 points = €100 voucher (fixed reward option).

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Conversion Redemption Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rewards can be redeemed in flexible amounts, calculated proportionally.

Comment:
Example: 1 point = €0.01, allowing any redemption amount.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Full Redemption Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption requires a complete set of points for a specific reward.

Comment:
Example: 5,000 points required for a gift item.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Bonus-Adjusted Conversion Redemption Calculation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The base calculation is enhanced by bonuses or multipliers.

Comment:
Example: 20% extra value during promotions or for premium customers.
